scenario: Atlas Insurance ships a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service that deploys 14 BPMN diagrams via the @Deployment annotation at startup, processing 22000 claims per day. The platform team has not configured camunda.client.deployment.enabled in any profile and must confirm what the 8.8 properties reference says about whether the annotation is processed.
question: How does camunda.client.deployment.enabled behave by default when left unset on a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service?

options.text: The properties reference lists camunda.client.deployment.enabled with a default of true so the @Deployment annotation is processed at startup with no extra configuration.
The properties reference lists camunda.client.deployment.enabled with a default of false so the @Deployment annotation is ignored until an operator opts in.
The properties reference lists camunda.client.deployment.enabled with a default of null so the annotation is processed only when camunda.client.mode is saas.
The properties reference lists camunda.client.deployment.enabled with a default of true so the diagrams deploy continuously while the application is running in 8.8.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.deployment section documents enabled as a boolean with a default value of true; the @Deployment annotation is processed at startup unless the property is set to false.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented default is true, not false; the @Deployment annotation is processed unless the property is explicitly overridden.

c.text: Incorrect. The default is true, not null, and there is no documented coupling between deployment.enabled and camunda.client.mode in the reference.

d.text: Incorrect. The annotation is processed at startup only; the property does not enable a continuous redeployment loop while the application runs.

explanation: The 8.8 properties reference documents camunda.client.deployment.enabled with a default value of true. It controls whether the @Deployment annotation is processed when the Spring Boot application starts.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
